Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Yuesen Yuen And Defendants Counsel Karen Uchiyama
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Laura Flynn, Esq. of Hooshmand Law Group, Tel. No. 415.318.5709, appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Tyson Redenbarger, Esq. of Hooshmand Law Group, Tel. No. 415.318.5709, appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Curtis F. Dowling, Esq. of Dowling & Marquez LLP, Tel. No. 415.977.044 x233, appearing for the Defendant.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with modifications on Plaintiffs Kelly Creps and Shamsi Creps' Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Yuesen Yuen And Defendants Counsel Karen Uchiyama as follows: Plaintiffs' unopposed motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory 15.1, Requests for Production 7, 8 and 41, and Special Interrogatories 5 and 19. The motion is granted in part as to Special Interrogatories 23 and 24 - Defendant need only identify responsive communications that concern when Defendant would be present at 238 Moultrie Street. Responses to be provided by April 11, 2018. The motion is denied as to Form Interrogatory 17.1 (Plaintiff has not provided the Requests for Admission or Defendant's responses so the Court cannot evaluate Defendant's responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1), Requests for Production 29 and 45 (Defendant's response is that she will comply with the requests; if she fails to do so, Plaintiffs' remedy is a motion to compel compliance under CCP 2031.320); and Request for Production 44 (the request is overbroad). Discovery sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,000. Payment of sanctions must be made within fourteen (14) days of the entry of the order. Sanctions waived if there is full compliance. Order signed as modified.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, Tel. No. 415.828.1919. =(302/JPT/HEK)
